creator: namor@hemio.de
text:
On Sun, Jul 14, 2013 at 11:04:45PM +0100, Gabor Pali wrote:
> Hi Roman,
> 
> I think your patch is not needed.  Yesod 1.2 has merged the
> functionality of yesod-default [1] into yesod [2] -- hence git-annex
> should just work by using that package only.  I have created a patch
> for fixing this up in git-annex package description [3] (*) I believe
> that is the way of resolving the situation, but I have not yet come to
> test it properly.

Thanks for your work.

I tested your patch (including the extra file you gave me via IRC) and it
works perfectly on my amd64 9.1-p4 system.

Note that it suffices to update the hs-git-annex port alone, the updates from the haskell
overlay on github (to yesod or other ports) are not necessary to fix hs-git-annex.

In other words: From the haskell ports overlay [3], I copied the devel/hs-git-annex port's directory into my (official) /usr/ports tree. After that, I added the patch-Yesod.hs file your gave me via IRC.

The result builds and runs fine with the WEBAPP option enabled, with the caveat (as we discussed via IRC as well) that you have to run |make config| manually to disable DYNAMIC (if WEBAPP is enabled).

To allow others to test the port update (and possibly to make committing easier), here is the whole patch (from [3] + your patch file from IRC). As before, apply with -p1:

  # cd /usr/ports
  # patch -p1 < /path/to/patch.txt

> [3] https://raw.github.com/freebsd-haskell/ports/master/devel/hs-git-annex/files/patch-git-annex.cabal
